UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Stores user-specific information, including their role within the IntelliSecureX system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

email.description: The user's email address, used for authentication and contact.

role.description: The access role of the user within the system (e.g., 'Admin', 'User').

createdAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the user profile was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Stores metadata about a document uploaded to the IntelliSecureX system. The actual document content is stored in Firebase Storage.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

name.description: The original name of the uploaded document.

storagePath.description: The path or URI to the original document file in Firebase Storage.

ownerId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the user who uploaded this document. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document)

uploadedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the document was uploaded.

decoyGeneratedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when the decoy version of the document was last generated or updated.

originalContentTextPath.description: Path to the extracted plain text content of the original document in Firebase Storage, used for NLP processing.

decoyContentTextPath.description: Path to the plain text content of the decoy document in Firebase Storage.

ActivityLog.title: ActivityLog
ActivityLog.description: Records significant user and system activities within the IntelliSecureX platform for monitoring and auditing purposes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the ActivityLog entry.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile entity of the user who performed the action, if applicable. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N ActivityLog)

actionType.description: The type of action performed (e.g., 'User authenticated', 'Document uploaded', 'Decoy activated', 'Unauthorized access attempt').

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity involved in the action, if applicable. (Relationship: Document 1:N ActivityLog)

timestamp.description: Timestamp indicating when the action occurred.

status.description: The outcome or state of the action (e.g., 'success', 'failed', 'info', 'warning').

description.description: A more detailed textual description of the activity.

DocumentAnalysisResult.title: DocumentAnalysisResult
DocumentAnalysisResult.description: Stores the results of the NLP detection and decoy generation for sensitive entities found within a specific document.

id.description: Unique identifier for this specific document analysis result.

documentId.description: Reference to the Document entity this analysis result belongs to. (Relationship: Document 1:N DocumentAnalysisResult)

entityType.description: The type of sensitive entity detected (e.g., 'PERSON_NAME', 'PAN_NUMBER', 'EMAIL').

originalValue.description: The actual sensitive value detected in the original document.

decoyValue.description: The replacement decoy value generated for the sensitive entity.

confidence.description: A numerical score (0.0 to 1.0) indicating the confidence level of the detection.

detectedAt.description: Timestamp indicating when this entity was detected and processed.

definition.description: Stores user-specific information, including their role (Admin/User) which is essential for authorization. Access is path-based: a user can only read/write their own profile.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Records significant user and system activities for monitoring and auditing. Includes 'userId' and 'documentId' for context and authorization independence. Primarily readable by Admins, users can read their own logs.

params.name: logId
params.description: The unique identifier for an activity log entry.

definition.description: Stores metadata for documents uploaded by a specific user. Includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field which matches the path's 'userId' for robust authorization independence and Collection Group Queries. Ensures users only access their own document metadata.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the document owner, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the document metadata entry.

definition.description: Stores NLP detection and decoy generation results for sensitive entities within a specific document. Access is inherited from and tied to the parent document's ownership, leveraging the 'userId' in the path for authorization independence.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who owns the parent document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent document.

params.name: resultId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific document analysis result.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for IntelliSecureX prioritizes robust security, scalability, and debuggability by strictly adhering to the core design principles. The structure leverages a hierarchical approach for user-owned data and top-level collections for global, system-wide data, enabling clear and concise security rules.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
This design mandates the denormalization of authorization context to eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules, thereby supporting atomic operations and simplifying rule debugging. Here's how it's achieved:

1.  **`/userProfiles/{userId}`:** Access to a user's profile is directly tied to the authenticated user's ID (`request.auth.uid == userId`), ensuring immediate authorization without external lookups.
2.  **`/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}`:** The `Document` entity includes an explicit `ownerId` field. While the `userId` in the path already denotes ownership, including `ownerId` in the document itself serves as a crucial denormalized authorization field. This allows security rules to evaluate `request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId` directly for `get` and `list` operations, ensuring atomicity and facilitating potential Collection Group Queries if needed to list all documents across all users. This eliminates any dependency on retrieving parent document data for authorization.
3.  **`/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}/analysisResults/{resultId}`:** `DocumentAnalysisResult` documents are nested directly under their respective `Document` and `User` paths. Their access is implicitly governed by the parent `Document`'s ownership. The rule `request.auth.uid == userId` (where `userId` is the path parameter) naturally provides authorization independence for these subcollection documents.
4.  **`/activityLogs/{logId}`:** Each `ActivityLog` document includes a `userId` field. For a user to access their own activity logs, the rule `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` can be applied without requiring a `get()` call to a user profile. Admin access to all logs is based on their `role` in `userProfiles`, which is a single `get()` to their own profile, not a hierarchical dependency for `activityLogs` themselves.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
The structural segregation and denormalization strategy ensure that security rules are not used as filters, allowing for efficient and secure `list` operations:

1.  **`/userProfiles/{userId}`:** A user can `get` their own profile directly. Admins, identified by their `role` in `userProfiles/{adminUid}`, can `list` all user profiles efficiently, without filtering.
2.  **`/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}`:** Users can perform `list` queries on *their own* documents (`/users/{{request.auth.uid}}/documents`). This is a direct query without server-side filtering. For admins, `Collection Group Queries` on the `documents` collection can be used, with the `ownerId` field enabling an efficient rule `allow list: if isAdmin() || request.auth.uid == resource.data.ownerId;`. This avoids filtering results after retrieval.
3.  **`/users/{userId}/documents/{documentId}/analysisResults/{resultId}`:** Similar to documents, users can `list` analysis results for *their own* documents, as the path inherently scopes the query to their owned data.
4.  **`/activityLogs/{logId}`:** Admins can `list` *all* activity logs directly from the root collection. If users are allowed to view only their own logs, a query can be made with `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`, which is an indexed query and not a rule-based filter.
